_README: COPY THIS FILE TO 'appsettings.json' IN THE SAME FOLDER, THEN EDIT THE PATHS.
This file is named .Example.json on purpose: ASP.NET Core loads only appsettings.json and
appsettings.{Environment}.json, so this one is inert. It exists to be copied, not to take effect.
All three paths below are REQUIRED (M11). The app refuses to start if any is missing, and tells you
which one. That is deliberate: before M11 an unset DbPath silently fell through to the desktop app's
%APPDATA% defaults, so an operator got a running app pointed at the WRONG database with no signal.
DbPath: if a database already exists at this path it is OPENED as-is. If nothing is there, a fresh
one is created and seeded with an 'admin' / 'admin' account. Change that password immediately -
there is a Change Password screen in the sidebar under ACCOUNT.
ConfigPath: where the WRITABLE policy store lives (backup folder, retention window, export roots).
It does NOT have to exist - it is created on first write. It must NOT be this file.
KeyRingPath: Data Protection keys. These must OUTLIVE the process - losing them logs everyone out.
Put them somewhere that gets backed up.
Every one of these can also be set as an environment variable, e.g. TimesheetApp__DbPath.
The startup banner prints the paths that actually WON, every start. Read it.

TimesheetApp._ArchivePath: Optional. Legacy flat standup/tasklist archives. Omit for the built-in default.
TimesheetApp._SeedFirstAdmin: Optional, defaults to true. Set false ONLY if you provision accounts out of band - a fresh database with it off is unloggable-into.
